title: Exodus: Wilderness Provision
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of exodus: wilderness provision and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study exodus: wilderness provision and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What food did God provide daily for Israel in the wilderness, and what rule governed how much they could collect on the sixth day? Answer: manna; on the sixth day they were to collect a double portion because none would fall on the Sabbath.
segments.correct_answer: manna; on the sixth day they collected double because none fell on the Sabbath
acceptable_variants: bread from heaven called manna, with double portion on the sixth day
manna each morning; twice as much on friday before the sabbath
god sent manna and forbade collecting on the sabbath

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The bread-like substance God provided daily to feed Israel in the wilderness for forty years; Jesus references it in John 6 as he presents himself as the true bread from heaven
The birds God sent when Israel complained about the absence of meat in the wilderness; provided abundantly but followed by a plague when eaten with greed
